instruction: My eyesight is getting worse. Please make the system significantly easier to read by doing all of the following: enable Large Text (text scaling >= 1.2), set cursor size to at least 48, and enable screen magnifier with magnification factor > 1.5.

command: import pyautogui; import time; pyautogui.click({SCREEN_WIDTH_HALF}, {SCREEN_HEIGHT_HALF}); time.sleep(0.5);

result.command: TEXT_SCALE=$(gsettings get org.gnome.desktop.interface text-scaling-factor 2>/dev/null | grep -oE '[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?' | head -1); CURSOR_SIZE=$(gsettings get org.gnome.desktop.interface cursor-size 2>/dev/null | grep -oE '[0-9]+' | head -1); MAG_ENABLED=$(gsettings get org.gnome.desktop.a11y.applications screen-magnifier-enabled 2>/dev/null); MAG_FACTOR=$(gsettings get org.gnome.desktop.a11y.magnifier mag-factor 2>/dev/null | grep -oE '[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?' | head -1); if [ -n "$TEXT_SCALE" ] && [ -n "$CURSOR_SIZE" ] && [ "$MAG_ENABLED" = "true" ] && [ -n "$MAG_FACTOR" ] && [ "$(echo "$TEXT_SCALE >= 1.2" | bc -l 2>/dev/null || echo 0)" = "1" ] && [ "$CURSOR_SIZE" -ge 48 ] && [ "$(echo "$MAG_FACTOR > 1.5" | bc -l 2>/dev/null || echo 0)" = "1" ]; then echo PASS; else echo FAIL; fi
